readOnlyUserDescription: Databasus will use a read-only user to perform backups
cpuCountDescription: Number of CPUs to use for backup operations
includeSchemasDescription: Select schemas to include in backup
failedToReadClipboard: Failed to read clipboard. Please check browser permissions.
youCanContinueWithNextStep: You can continue with next step
skipBackingUpEvents: Skip backing up database events. Enable this if event scheduler is disabled on your MariaDB server.
ifYourDatabaseUsesIpWhitelist: If your database uses IP whitelist, make sure Databasus server IP is added to allowed list.
toStudyHowToBackupLocalDatabase: to study how to backup local database
skipExtensionsDescription: Skip restoring extension definitions (CREATE EXTENSION statements). Enable this if you're restoring to a managed PostgreSQL service where extensions are managed by the provider.
toStudyHowToBackupSupabaseDatabase: to study how to backup Supabase database
deleteDatabaseConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this database? This action cannot be undone.
transferDatabaseDescription: Transfer this database to another workspace
storageUsedByDatabases: This storage is used by {{count}} databases. Transfer is blocked because other databases depend on it.
willNotBeTransferred: Will NOT be transferred (used by other databases):
noNotifiersTransferred: No notifiers will be transferred. You can select notifiers from the target workspace after transfer.
storageDescription: Storage - is a place where backups will be stored (local disk, S3, Google Drive, etc.)
notifierDescription: Notifier - is a place where notifications will be sent (email, Slack, Telegram, etc.)
notifierInfo: Notifier - is a place where notifications will be sent (email, Slack, Telegram, etc.)
notifierInfo2: You can select several notifiers, notifications will be sent to all of them.
databaseTransferredDescription: "{{name}}" has been transferred to the new workspace
testConnectionButton: test connection via button below (even if you updated settings);
waitNextBackup: wait until next backup is done without errors;
copyDatabaseConfirmation: Are you sure you want to copy this database? A new database with same settings will be created.
removeDatabaseConfirmation: Are you sure you want to remove this database? This action cannot be undone.
createReadOnlyUserTitle: Create a read-only user for Databasus?
createReadOnlyUserDescription: A read-only user is a {{type}} user with limited permissions that can only read data from your database, not modify it. This is recommended for backup operations because:
createReadOnlyUserReason1: it prevents accidental data modifications during backup
createReadOnlyUserReason2: it follows principle of least privilege
createReadOnlyUserSecurityEnd: ). However, it is not possible to be covered from all possible risks.
createReadOnlyUserWarning: Read-only user allows to avoid storing credentials with write access at all. Even in worst case of hacking, nobody will be able to corrupt your data.
skipReadOnlyUserConfirmation: Are you sure you want to skip creating a read-only user?
skipReadOnlyUserWarning: Using a user with full permissions for backups is not recommended and may pose security risks. Databasus is highly recommending you to not skip this step.
skipReadOnlyUserRisk: 100% protection is never possible. It's better to be safe in case of 0.01% risk of full hacking. So it is better to follow secure way with read-only user.
enableHealthcheckTooltip: Enable or disable healthcheck monitoring for this database
notifyWhenUnavailableTooltip: Send notifications when database becomes unavailable
checkIntervalMinutesTooltip: How often to check database health (in minutes)
attemptsBeforeDownTooltip: Number of failed attempts before marking database as down
storeAttemptsDaysTooltip: How many days to store health check attempt history
sshSkipHostKeyVerifyDescription: Skip SSH host key verification (not recommended for production)
